issues.main_description_html:
RESULT 25/06/26

✅ Implementation: API Add Affiliate Category — Ready for Unit Testing

Example API Add Affiliate Category

Endpoint: POST /support/affiliate/category
Description: Creates a new affiliate category. Fails if the user does not have the support role or if the category name already exists.

Example cURL Request

curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/support/affiliate/category \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <your_token>" \
  -d '{
    "name": "E-Commerce"
  }'

Request Payload

{
  "name": "E-Commerce"
}

Success Response

{
  "statusCode": 200,
  "data": {
    "valid": true,
    "messages": "Affiliate category created successfully",
    "id": 4,
    "position": 4
  }
}

Error Responses

Forbidden (User is not a support account):

{
  "statusCode": 403,
  "data": {
    "error": "Permission denied"
  }
}

Bad Request (Category name already exists or name is empty):

{
  "statusCode": 400,
  "data": {
    "error": "Category name already exists"
  }
}

Implementation Summary: Add Affiliate Category

This document details the exact changes and logic implemented to support the Create API for Affiliate Categories.

Overview

  • Goal: Create an endpoint to add an affiliate category.
  • Constraints: Only accessible by accounts with the support role.

Files Created & Modified

1. src/Action/V1/Supports/AddAffiliateCategory.go (New File)

  • Purpose: Handles the creation of new affiliate categories.
  • Logic Breakdown:
    1. Role Check: Verifies if c.Locals("role") == "support". If not, returns 403 Forbidden.
    2. Validation: Checks if the provided name is not empty.
    3. Duplicate Check: Queries the affiliate_categories table to ensure no category with the same name (case-insensitive) exists. Returns 400 Bad Request if a duplicate is found.
    4. Position Allocation: Queries SELECT COALESCE(MAX(position), 0) + 1 FROM affiliate_categories to determine the next position for the newly created category so it appears at the end of the list.
    5. Insertion: Relies on the database for id auto-increment (AUTO_INCREMENT). Stores the title-cased (Ucwords) name and the generated position into the affiliate_categories table.

2. src/Controllers/V1/SupportsController.go (Modified)

  • Purpose: Bridges the HTTP Route to the newly created Action.
  • Modifications: Added the AddAffiliateCategory controller function: 
    func AddAffiliateCategory(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
    return SupportsAction.AddAffiliateCategory(c)
}

3. src/Routes/V1/SupportsRoutes.go (Modified)

  • Purpose: Registers the HTTP methods and URL paths.
  • Modifications: Registered the POST endpoint inside the support route group: 
    Support.Post("/affiliate-category", Controllers.AddAffiliateCategory)

API Documentation

The API documentation details for testing with tools like Postman/cURL can be found in:

  • docs/API/add_affiliate_category.md

This API creates a new affiliate category. It is restricted to users with the support role.

Endpoint

POST /api/v1/support/affiliate-category

Request

Headers

  • Authorization: Bearer <token>
  • Content-Type: application/json

Body

{
  "name": "Category Name"
}

Response

Success (200 OK)

{
  "statusCode": 200,
  "data": {
    "valid": true,
    "messages": "Affiliate category created successfully",
    "id": 4
  }
}

Error: Forbidden (403)

If the user does not have the support role:

{
  "statusCode": 403,
  "message": "Permission denied"
}

Error: Bad Request (400)

If the name already exists or is empty:

{
  "statusCode": 400,
  "message": "Category name already exists"
}

RESULT 24/06/26

✅ Implementation: API Update Affiliate Category — Ready for Unit Testing

Example API Update Affiliate Category

Endpoint: PUT /support/affiliate/category
Description: Updates an existing affiliate category. Fails if the user does not have the support role, if the category is currently in use by an affiliate, or if the new name conflicts with an existing category.

Example cURL Request

curl -X PUT http://localhost:3000/support/affiliate/category \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <your_token>" \
  -d '{
    "id": 1,
    "name": "Updated E-Commerce"
  }'

Request Payload

{
  "id": 1,
  "name": "Updated E-Commerce"
}

Success Response

{
  "statusCode": 200,
  "data": {
    "valid": true,
    "messages": "Affiliate category updated successfully"
  }
}

Error Responses

Forbidden (User is not a support account):

{
  "statusCode": 403,
  "data": {
    "error": "Permission denied"
  }
}

Not Found (Category ID does not exist):

{
  "statusCode": 404,
  "data": {
    "error": "Category not found"
  }
}

Bad Request (Category is already in use by one or more affiliates):

{
  "statusCode": 400,
  "data": {
    "error": "Cannot update category because it is already in use by one or more affiliates"
  }
}

Bad Request (New category name already exists):

{
  "statusCode": 400,
  "data": {
    "error": "Category name already exists"
  }
}

This document details the exact changes and logic implemented to support the Update API for Affiliate Categories.

Overview

  • Goal: Create an endpoint to update an existing affiliate category.
  • Constraints:
    1. Only accessible by accounts with the support role.
    2. The update process must fail if the category is currently being used in the affiliate table.

Files Created & Modified

1. src/Action/V1/Supports/UpdateAffiliateCategory.go (New File)

  • Purpose: Handles updating existing affiliate categories.
  • Logic Breakdown:
    1. Role Check: Verifies if c.Locals("role") == "support". Returns 403 Forbidden if it fails.
    2. Validation: Validates input parameters for both id and name.
    3. Existence Check: Verifies if the category with the given ID exists. Returns 404 Not Found if it doesn't.
    4. In-Use Check (Constraint): Checks the affiliate table COUNT where category_id matches the request ID. If COUNT > 0, it returns 400 Bad Request with an error stating the category is currently in use.
    5. Duplicate Conflict Check: Verifies if the new name clashes with another existing category (filtering out the current ID). Uses LOWER(name) = LOWER(?) to ensure the check is case-insensitive.
    6. Update Execution: Updates the name and sets updated_at to the current time.

2. src/Controllers/V1/SupportsController.go (Modified)

  • Purpose: Bridges the HTTP Route to the newly created Action.
  • Modifications: Added the UpdateAffiliateCategory controller function: 
    func UpdateAffiliateCategory(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
    return SupportsAction.UpdateAffiliateCategory(c)
}

3. src/Routes/V1/SupportsRoutes.go (Modified)

  • Purpose: Registers the HTTP methods and URL paths.
  • Modifications: Registered the PUT endpoint inside the support route group: 
    Support.Put("/affiliate-category", Controllers.UpdateAffiliateCategory)

API Documentation

The API documentation details for testing with tools like Postman/cURL can be found in:

  • docs/API/update_affiliate_category.md

This API updates an existing affiliate category. It is restricted to users with the support role.

Endpoint

PUT /api/v1/support/affiliate-category

Request

Headers

  • Authorization: Bearer <token>
  • Content-Type: application/json

Body

{
  "id": 1,
  "name": "Updated Category Name"
}

Response

Success (200 OK)

{
  "statusCode": 200,
  "data": {
    "valid": true,
    "messages": "Affiliate category updated successfully"
  }
}

Error: Forbidden (403)

If the user does not have the support role:

{
  "statusCode": 403,
  "message": "Permission denied"
}

Error: Not Found (404)

If the category ID does not exist:

{
  "statusCode": 404,
  "message": "Category not found"
}

Error: Bad Request (400)

If the category is already in use or the new name conflicts with an existing category:

{
  "statusCode": 400,
  "message": "Cannot update category because it is already in use by one or more affiliates"
}

or

{
  "statusCode": 400,
  "message": "Category name already exists"
}

RESULT 24/06/26

✅ Implementation: API List Affiliate Categories — Ready for Unit Testing

Example API Update Affiliate Category

List Affiliate Categories API

Endpoint: GET /affiliate/categories/list
Description: Retrieves a paginated list of affiliate categories. Supports search by category name. The response format and data visibility dynamically adapt based on the requester's role (user vs support).

Example cURL Request

curl -G "http://localhost:3000/affiliate/categories/list" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <your_token>" \
  --data-urlencode "page=1" \
  --data-urlencode "limit=20" \
  --data-urlencode "search=Commerce"

Request Parameters

  • page (int, optional): Page number for pagination. Defaults to 1.
  • limit (int, optional): Number of items per page. Defaults to 20.
  • search (string, optional): Keyword to search categories by name.

Success Response (User Role)

Note: For the user role, only categories with is_active = true are returned, and only the id and name fields are visible.

{
  "statusCode": 200,
  "data": {
    "valid": true,
    "version": 0,
    "messages": "List Affiliate Categories",
    "total": 1,
    "data_category": [
      {
        "id": 4,
        "name": "E-Commerce"
      }
    ]
  }
}

Success Response (Support Role)

Note: For the support role, all categories are returned (regardless of is_active status) with all fields visible.

{
  "statusCode": 200,
  "data": {
    "valid": true,
    "version": 0,
    "messages": "List Affiliate Categories",
    "total": 1,
    "data_category": [
      {
        "id": 4,
        "name": "E-Commerce",
        "is_active": true,
        "position": 4,
        "created_at": "2023-10-25T10:00:00Z",
        "updated_at": "2023-10-25T10:00:00Z"
      }
    ]
  }
}

Error Responses

Unauthorized (User context invalid or missing):

{
  "statusCode": 401,
  "data": {
    "error": "Unauthorized"
  }
}

Internal Server Error (Database connection failure):

{
  "statusCode": 500,
  "data": {
    "error": "Internal Server Error"
  }
}

This document explains how the "API List Affiliate Categories" task was implemented within the Panorra GET Project architecture.

1. Route Registration

  • File: src/Routes/V1/AffiliateRoutes.go
  • Details: The route is grouped under the /affiliate prefix. The endpoint /categories/list is mapped to the ListAffiliateCategories controller.

2. Controller Wrapper

  • File: src/Controllers/V1/AffiliateControllers.go
  • Details: A thin controller ListAffiliateCategories(c *fiber.Ctx) intercepts the HTTP request and delegates the business logic to the Action layer.

3. Business Logic (Action Layer)

  • File: src/Action/V1/Affiliate/ListAffiliateCategories.go
  • Details:
    • Context Extraction: Retrieves the role, ec (encryption flag), and encrypt strings from the Fiber context. It also extracts user information like lastLogin.
    • Query Building: Constructs dynamic Gorm SQL queries against the affiliate_categories table.
    • Pagination & Search: Applies OFFSET, LIMIT, and ILIKE condition for search filtering.
    • Role Logic:
      • Appends .Where("is_active = ?", true) strictly for the user role.
      • Uses a customized mapping to iterate over the queried results and construct an array of AffiliateCategoryUser structs (id and name only) for the user role, while returning the full slice of AffiliateCategory structs for the support role.
    • Encryption: Automatically encrypts the JSON response body if the client specifies ec=true.

4. Database Models

  • File: src/Models/AffiliateModel.go
  • Details: The existing Affiliate model file was extended to include the structures for the categories table:
    • AffiliateCategory: Maps all columns (id, name, is_active, position, created_at, updated_at) for the support role and query scanning.
    • AffiliateCategoryUser: A minimal struct mapping only id and name for the user role response projection.

5. API Documentation

  • File: docs/API.md
  • Details: The new endpoint, along with its query parameters and role-based response rules, has been documented under the ## Affiliate section.

Objective

Create a new read-only API endpoint to retrieve a list of Affiliate Categories. The endpoint needs to support basic listing, pagination, and searching by category name. The response payload and filtering logic must dynamically adapt based on the requester's role (user vs support).

Requirements

  • Endpoint: GET /affiliate/categories/list
  • Pagination: support standard page and limit query parameters.
  • Search: support a search query parameter to filter categories by name, case-insensitive.
  • Role-based access control:
    • User role:
      • Can only retrieve categories where is_active is true.
      • Can only see id and name fields in the response.
    • Support role:
      • Can retrieve all categories, regardless of is_active status.
      • Can see all fields: id, name, is_active, position, created_at, updated_at.

Source

  • docs/task/Affiliate_Categories_API.md

Project Metadata

  • Task order: #9899
